title: Hand Replay
description: Visualize each action step by step with equity evaluation and decision analysis.
noHands: No hands found for this tournament.
noParams: No hand selected. Return to the tournament and click 'Open in replayer'.

icm.riskTip: Final table: you hold one of the bigger stacks, by ICM your chips are worth less than their prize-pool share (diminishing returns). Avoid marginal flips.
icm.survivalTip: Final table: short stack with a survival premium, your ICM equity exceeds your chip share. Be selective about risking elimination.
icm.neutralTip: Final table: balanced stacks, light ICM pressure, but every all-in still risks elimination.

card.stackTip: Hero's effective stack in big blinds
card.mTip: M-Ratio: stack ÷ average orbit cost. M < 10 = pressure, M < 5 = critical zone
card.icmTip: ICM pressure: impact of eliminations on tournament expected value
card.tipNoCoverage: This spot is outside GTO coverage (e.g. a limped pot). There is no solver verdict, the review doesn't fabricate one.
card.tipGtoSolver: GTO Solver: Nash equilibrium frequencies computed for this specific spot
card.tipRange: Preflop: GTO opening ranges by position and stack depth
card.tipEngine: Engine: estimated equity, M-Ratio, ICM pressure and tournament context
card.gtoCorrectTip: Optimal action: GTO frequency ≥ 60%, correct play per the solver
card.gtoMixedTip: Mixed strategy: GTO frequency 30–60%, valid action, but not dominant in the range
card.gtoMinorTip: Minor deviation: GTO frequency 10–30%, infrequent in GTO, but defensible
card.gtoCriticalTip: Critical deviation: GTO frequency < 10%, rarely justified by the solver
card.evLossTip: EV lost vs the best play for this hand, how many big blinds you left on the table.
card.srcGtoTip: GTO Solver: Nash equilibrium frequencies computed for this spot
card.srcPreflopTip: Preflop: GTO opening ranges by position and stack depth
card.srcEngineTip: Engine: estimated equity, M-Ratio, ICM pressure and tournament context
card.srcHeuristicTip: Heuristic analysis: the solver has no coverage for this multiway spot
card.srcPushfoldTip: Push/Fold zone: stack ≤12bb, binary shove-or-fold decision
card.srcNaTip: Incompatible spot: solver was queried for a spot different from the real one
card.handRangeTip: Shows whether your hand is in the scenario's GTO range, not whether your ACTION was correct (that's the verdict at the top of the card). A hand can be in range and still be played wrong.
card.v2ToggleTip: Switches between the classic card and the new v2 layout (bb cost as the headline). Preference is saved in this browser.
card.v2CostSub: cost of this decision (EV of your hand)
card.v2MaxEv: max EV: your hand had no better play
card.handStratTip: Frequency and EV of this specific hand at the solver node, the bars above are the average of the whole range. The bb value shows what each action yields (amber = cost vs the hand's best action).
card.mwDisclaimer: Solver-independent heuristic: hand equity vs continuation range (Monte Carlo) + pot odds + multiway realization adjustment. Estimate, not GTO.
card.tipMultiwayEstimate: Multiway pot: the solver is heads-up and doesn't solve 3-way+. Here the verdict comes from an independent estimate, your hand's equity vs the opponents' continuing range (Monte Carlo) + pot odds + a realization adjustment. It's an estimate, not GTO.
card.whyMultiwayEstimate: Multiway pot: the solver is heads-up. The verdict comes from an estimate, your hand's equity vs the opponents' continuing range, against the pot odds, with a realization adjustment for the number of players.
card.reqVsRandomTip: Equity estimated vs a random hand, not vs the opponent's actual range. The verdict comes from your hand's GTO strategy (above). Vs the opponent's range, equity is usually lower.
card.reqVsRangeTip: Equity estimated vs the opponent's actual GTO opening range (not vs a random hand), from the hand-vs-hand equity matrix. The verdict comes from your hand's GTO strategy (above).
card.reqSolverContextTip: Pot odds of your action. The verdict comes from the solver strategy (above), which accounts for the whole range and future streets, not just this calculation. That's why the margin is shown neutral.
card.depthCappedTip: Approximate GTO: in very deep spots (above ~60bb) the solver returns an approximation. Direction is reliable; exact frequencies may vary.

betIntentTip.value_showdown: Your hand is strong. You bet so that worse hands call and you profit from them.
betIntentTip.value_protection: Your hand is ahead, but the board can give your opponent a draw. Betting charges them to continue and protects your hand (only makes sense on the flop and turn).
betIntentTip.semi_bluff: You don't have a made hand yet, but you have a draw (flush or straight) that can complete. The bet can make your opponent fold now, and if they call, you still have cards to improve.
betIntentTip.middle: A medium-strength hand: good enough to beat a bluff at showdown, but too weak to bet for value. If you bet, worse hands fold and only better hands call, so you only get called when you're behind. Most of the time, checking is better.
betIntentTip.bluff: Your hand is probably behind. You bet to make your opponent fold a better hand.

card.villainSampleLowTip: Few hands seen from this player, no reliable read yet. Reads only appear with enough sample.

archetypeHint.calling_station: Calls too much. Bluffs work less; value-bet thinner and give up the bluffs.
archetypeHint.nit: Very tight and folds a lot. Respect his aggression; bluff more and pay off less.
archetypeHint.tag: Solid (tight-aggressive). Plays close to GTO, few leaks to exploit.
archetypeHint.lag: Loose and aggressive. Call wider; careful not to over-fold to his pressure.
archetypeHint.maniac: Hyper-aggressive. Call and re-raise wider for value; let him bluff.

exploit.dont_bluff_station: Calling station: don't bluff here. Even if GTO bluffs, against him worse hands don't fold: the bluff is −EV. Bet for value only.
exploit.value_thicker_station: Calling station: bet bigger and thinner for value. He pays off too much; thin value prints more than vs GTO.
exploit.station_bets_strength: Careful: a passive player betting usually has a strong hand. Fold marginals you'd call vs an aggressor.
exploit.call_wider_aggro: He's very aggressive / bluffs a lot (AF {{af}}). Call wider and don't over-fold to his pressure.
exploit.overfold_nit: Nit: tight betting range, rarely bluffs. Fold your marginals here.
exploit.bluff_more_nit: Folding nit: you can bluff more. He gives up too much; cheap bluffs profit.

card.rationaleTitle: Why this is the best play

rationale.check_marginal: Weak/medium hand ({{eq}}%) with no strong draw. Betting doesn't extract value (worse hands fold, better ones call) and there's no profitable bluff, checking controls the pot.
rationale.check_marginal_mw: Weak/medium hand ({{eq}}%) with no strong draw, in a {{n}}-way pot. Betting doesn't extract value (worse hands fold, better ones call) and bluffing into several players rarely works, checking controls the pot.
rationale.check_strong: Strong hand: checking can induce a bet from the villain or control the pot size (the board doesn't need protection).
rationale.bet_value: Strong hand ({{eq}}%): value bet, you want worse hands to call.
rationale.bet_protection: Strong hand, but the board is wet: bet for value and protect, charging the villain's draws.
rationale.bet_semibluff: Strong draw: semi-bluff, fold equity now, and outs to complete if called.
rationale.bet_thin: Thin value bet: extracts from slightly worse hands, with caution.
rationale.call_odds: Equity {{eq}}% covers the pot odds (~{{req}}% required): calling has value.
rationale.fold_no_odds: Equity {{eq}}% below the ~{{req}}% the pot requires: no price to continue.
rationale.raise_value: Strong hand: raise for value and protection, building the pot.
rationale.raise_semibluff: Strong draw: raise as a semi-bluff, fold equity + outs.
rationale.shove_commit: Short stack or committed pot: shoving maximizes fold equity and realizes your equity with no tough decisions later.

noCoverage.multiway: Multiway pot: even when you open the action, you're betting into 2+ players, equity and responses are multiway. The solver is heads-up and can't model that, so the verdict here is heuristic, with equity adjusted for the number of opponents.
noCoverage.deep: Stack above 200bb: extreme depth, beyond even the solver's approximation. Heuristic verdict until we have a larger server.
noCoverage.ip_facing_bet: You're in position facing a bet: the solver's IP support only covers the c-bet (when the action reaches you with no bet). Heuristic verdict here.
noCoverage.no_villain: No defined villain to build the opposing range, the verdict is heuristic.

card.multiwaySolverTip: Multiway pot ({{n}} players in the pot). The solver strategy is solved heads-up, multiway it's an approximation: frequencies and ranges shift with more opponents. Equity is already adjusted for multiway.
card.multiwayTip: Multiway pot ({{n}} players in the pot). Equity is adjusted for the number of opponents; the verdict here is heuristic (pot odds).
card.limpedPot: {{pos}} vs Limp · limped pot · heuristic (no GTO range)
card.limpedPotTip: Limped pot (limp with no raise): a tree outside GTO coverage (we only cover raise-first spots: RFI/vs-RFI/3bet/squeeze). The verdict here comes from the engine heuristic (passive OK, isolating marginal), not the solver.
card.sprTip: SPR (Stack-to-Pot Ratio): effective stack ÷ pot. < 2 = committed; 2–5 = medium; > 5 = deep
card.sizingTip: Your bet size relative to the pot before the action
card.equityTip: Estimated equity of your hand vs the villain's likely range at this point
card.reqTipImplicit: Minimum equity for your own bet to be +EV (bet ÷ post-call pot). Your bet/raise threshold.
card.reqTipAdjusted: Minimum equity adjusted for realization and ICM pressure. Raw pot odds: {{potOdds}}%.
card.reqTipRaw: Minimum equity for the call to break even (bet ÷ (bet + pot))
card.whyMismatchFacing: The solver was queried for a spot with no bet, but you were facing a bet, analysis via engine heuristics.
card.whyMismatchNoBet: The solver was queried for a spot with a bet, but there was no bet, analysis via engine heuristics.
card.whyPushfold: Stack {{stack}}bb: push/fold dominant. Check the freq bar for the exact optimal action for this hand.
card.whyEngineConflict: Engine suggests {{engine}} but Solver indicates {{gto}}, we prioritize GTO.
card.whyFoldLeftEv: Fold left EV on the table, equity {{eqPct}}% beat {{reqLabel}} {{reqPct}}%, calling was preferable.
card.whyCheck: Check: equity {{eqPct}}% vs {{reqLabel}} {{reqPct}}% (no bet to face).
card.whyAggrProfit: {{act}} with equity {{eqPct}}% above the {{reqLabel}} {{reqPct}}%, profitable pressure.
card.whyAggrRisk: {{act}} with equity {{eqPct}}% below the {{reqLabel}} {{reqPct}}%, high risk without equity.
card.openOversizeCaveat: Note: villain opened {{facing}}bb (bigger than the GTO open ~{{canonical}}bb). The defense shown is vs the min-open, vs a larger open the correct defense is tighter, so folding marginal hands is defensible.
card.whyMultiway: Postflop spot with no precomputed solver solution, heuristic analysis with moderate confidence.
card.whyEqFavorable: Slightly favorable equity: play attentive to sizing.
card.whyEqWeak: Weak equity: tough spot, avoid bloating the pot.
card.statusRequesting: Analyzing this spot automatically. Reload the page shortly to see the results.
card.statusQueued: Queued: checking every 4s. Results will appear automatically.
card.statusSolverQueued: Spot queued for the solver, we don't have GTO data for this scenario yet. The computation may take a few minutes.
card.statusDoneNoSolution: The solver ran but returned no solution, postflop spot not covered in the current GTO tree.
card.statusError: Could not compute GTO for this spot.
card.quotaExceeded: You've reached your plan's monthly limit for on-demand GTO analyses.
card.quotaUpgradeHint: Upgrade your plan for unlimited analyses.
card.limpDeadMoney: ≈ push/fold · limp treated as dead money
card.limpDeadMoneyTip: Someone limped, but short-stacked a jam/fold over the limp is the SAME push/fold decision (the limp is just dead money, not a new GTO spot). Verdict from your push/fold range.

sizingAdvice.open_big: too big: standard is {{ideal}}; smaller risks less and controls the pot.
sizingAdvice.open_sb_small: vs BB, size up to {{ideal}}, a min-open gives the BB too good a price to complete.
sizingAdvice.open_iso_small: iso over a limp should be bigger ({{ideal}}).
sizingAdvice.postflop_too_big: too big: the solver uses ~{{gto}}% pot here
sizingAdvice.postflop_too_small: too small: the solver uses ~{{gto}}% pot here
sizingAdvice.3bet_big: {{pos}}: too big, standard is {{ideal}} the open
sizingAdvice.3bet_small: {{pos}}: too small, standard is {{ideal}} the open; a small 3-bet gives the open too good a price to call
sizingAdvice.tex_big: {{tex}} board: too big; standard is {{ideal}} pot
sizingAdvice.tex_small: {{tex}} board: too small; standard is {{ideal}} pot

threebetGloss.value_3bet: dominates the continuing range, wants chips in the middle
threebetGloss.merge_3bet: gets value from worse and denies equity, borderline
threebetGloss.light_3bet: little showdown: 3-bets for fold equity + blockers

threebetTip.value_3bet: Value 3-bet: your hand dominates the range that calls/4-bets. You want to build the pot and get chips in.
threebetTip.merge_3bet: Merge (thin-value) 3-bet: a borderline hand, gets value from worse hands that call and denies equity to those that fold. Careful vs very strong ranges.
threebetTip.light_3bet: Light (bluff) 3-bet: a low-showdown hand that 3-bets for fold equity and to block the villains value (e.g. A5s blocks AA/AK). It needs enough folds, vs a calling station it doesnt work.

sizingTextureTip.dry: Dry board (no draws): bet SMALL (~33%), range bet. No draws to charge, so bet cheap with your whole range.
sizingTextureTip.wet: Wet board (draws possible): bet BIGGER (~66%), charges the opponents draws and denies equity.
sizingTextureTip.very_wet: Very wet board (flush/straight possible): bet BIG (~85%+), polarize and charge the draws to the max.

card.whyLimped: Limped pot: the heuristic recommends a passive line. Check is the free option; isolating over the limp is optional.
card.approxDeep: ≈ Approximation (solver at {{n}}bb): your stack is deeper; the action is reliable, sizing may differ.
card.tipIcmApprox: Near the money, under ICM pressure, folding tight is often worth more than the chip EV suggests. This analysis is in chips (chipEV) and does not measure that risk premium, so a defensible fold here is not flagged as a mistake.

annotation.title: Coach annotation
annotation.placeholder: Add a note about this decision…
annotation.improveError: AI unavailable right now, try again.
annotation.label: Reclassify decision

hudToggleTip: Show/hide the player stats HUD on the table (VPIP/PFR/3-bet · hands). Holdem Manager style: only shown for players with a tracked sample.
hudLegend: Stats: VPIP: % hands played · PFR: % preflop raise · 3B: % 3-bet · cb: c-bet (continuation bet) · fcb: fold to c-bet · AF: postflop aggression factor · wtsd: % went to showdown · Nh: hands in sample. Blank value = insufficient sample for that stat.
iosInstallHint: For fullscreen on iPhone: tap Share, then "Add to Home Screen".
